canonicalSpecs.purpose: Repository root directory dictionary and .sdkwork workspace rules.
IAM domain boundary, package layering, persistence, and API contracts.
Third-party OAuth consumption and SDKWork OAuth provider authorization-server contracts.
Mandatory sdkwork-web-framework integration for HTTP *-api surfaces.
HTTP backend handler, service, repository, and route manifest rules.
Portable table contracts and schema evolution.
sdkwork-database lifecycle, migrations, seeds, and drift observation.
Drive-backed file upload ownership; IAM defers until avatar/media upload surfaces are added.
Service registry and RPC endpoint resolution; deferred until runnable IAM RPC servers ship.
gRPC/protobuf contracts owned under apis/rpc/iam/.
Local component specs directory and manifest rules.
Runtime configuration, environment, SDK bootstrap, and feature flag rules.
Module README, examples, ADR, changelog, and runbook rules.
Canonical domain ownership and naming.
UI, service, SDK, accessibility, and frontend runtime rules.
Standard ownership, exception, compatibility, and migration rules.
User-facing language, locale, message catalog, and fallback rules.
Reusable package contract and dependency direction.
SDKWork root standards entrypoint.
SDK generation and SDK integration rules.
Contract, frontend, SDK, security, parity, and documentation verification rules.
Authored source structure and generated code boundaries.
Canonical SDKWork naming rules.
TypeScript and Node package rules.
Frontend authored source structure.
UI architecture selection rules.
PC React package rules.
Generated SDK integration, TokenManager wiring, and dependency composition rules.
Login, session, AuthGate, and IAM runtime integration rules.
Token model, authn/authz, secrets, and secure logging rules.

integration.authority: Root SDKWork specs remain authoritative. Local specs may extend but must not contradict them.
integration.dependencyPolicy: Consumers integrate through public package exports, declared runtime entrypoints, generated SDK clients, or documented adapters only.
integration.sdkPolicy: Generated SDK clients are injected through service/runtime boundaries; reusable UI must not create raw HTTP clients or manual auth headers.
integration.languagePolicy: Language-specific packages follow their native manifest and build tools while preserving the same component contract.
integration.webFramework: IAM HTTP route crates integrate sdkwork-web-framework through sdkwork-web-axum and sdkwork-iam-web-adapter.
integration.database: IAM database module uses sdkwork-database lifecycle SPI via sdkwork-iam-database-host and sdkwork-iam-directory-repository-sqlx.
integration.utils: Rust IAM crates and PC React IAM packages use sdkwork-utils-rust and @sdkwork/utils for shared text helpers.
integration.discovery: Deferred until runnable IAM RPC servers register with sdkwork-discovery.
integration.drive: Not required; IAM has no file-upload surfaces. Avatar/media upload must use sdkwork-drive when added.

exceptions.reason: IAM owns RPC contracts and Rust service manifests, but defers sdkwork-discovery registration until runnable IAM RPC servers ship.
exceptions.scope: sdkwork-discovery integration for IAM RPC servers
